heroSubtitle: Chicago public data, explored through conversation.
suggestions: What's going on near 2400 N Milwaukee Ave?
Tell me about the property at 1425 N Wells St
Can I open a bar at 2200 W Chicago Ave?
Is Logan Square in a TIF district?

intelligence.heading: One question. Twenty-five sources.
intelligence.subheading: Every query pulls from property records, zoning regulations, tax estimates, incentive programs, transit data, demographics, crime, 311, permits, business licenses, and 14,535 sections of municipal code — simultaneously.

story.businessSubtitle: Check zoning compliance and 12 regulatory overlays, find TIF districts and incentive programs, review nearby competition through active business licenses, and understand the neighborhood — all from a single question.
story.residentTitle: Find the right place to live
story.residentSubtitle: Compare crime trends and arrest rates, check Walk Score and transit access, review property taxes and assessed values, see building violations and 311 patterns — the neighborhood report no listing gives you.

depth.heading: Ask one question. Get the full picture.
depth.subheading: Here's what UrbanLayer returns when you ask about a single Chicago address.

cards.title: Property dossier
cards.caption: Parcel data, 5-year assessment history, sales records, and tax estimates from Cook County.

cards.title: Regulatory profile
cards.caption: 12 overlay layers checked per address — landmarks to flood zones to ADU eligibility.

cards.title: Neighborhood snapshot
cards.caption: Demographics, transit access, and walkability scores assembled for every query.

explorer.heading: Explore a neighborhood
explorer.subheading: Pick a community area or enter an address to explore live city data — crime patterns, 311 service requests, building permits, and month-over-month trends, updated from the Chicago Data Portal.

personas.heading: Built for real decisions
personas.subheading: Whether you're evaluating a property, siting a business, or choosing a neighborhood.

items.title: The Investor
items.question: Tell me about the property at 1425 N Wells St
items.framing: Due diligence that used to take a week of FOIA requests.

items.title: The Business Owner
items.question: Can I open a restaurant at 2200 W Chicago Ave?
items.framing: Everything between your idea and your lease agreement.

items.title: The Resident
items.question: What's it like living near Damen and Division?
items.framing: The neighborhood report no listing gives you.

steps.title: You ask
steps.description: Ask in plain English — no query language, no filters to configure, no forms to fill out.
steps.query: Can I build a 6-flat at 2100 N Damen?

steps.title: We retrieve
steps.description: Your question activates the right combination of city, county, federal, and legal data sources — in parallel.

steps.title: You get answers
steps.description: A sourced, cited answer with interactive maps, trend analysis, and linked code references. Not a spreadsheet.

valueProps.knowBody: See what's happening around any Chicago address — crime patterns, open 311 requests, building permits, and zoning, all in one place.
valueProps.changingBody: Month-over-month trends show you whether crime is rising, construction is booming, or complaints are being resolved.
valueProps.answersBody: Ask questions in plain English. Our AI searches 14,000+ municipal code sections and 5 live city datasets to build your answer.

footer.tagline: Chicago public data, explored through conversation.
footer.cookCountyDesc: Property Characteristics, Assessed Values, Sales History, Tax Estimation
footer.federalExternalDesc: Census Demographics, FEMA Flood Zones, EPA Brownfields, Walk Score
footer.notAffiliated: Not affiliated with the City of Chicago
footer.dataDelay: Data may be delayed up to 7 days
